{"step_by_step_thinking": "Acid etching is commonly used in dentistry to prepare the tooth surface for bonding agents. One of the most significant advantages of acid etching is its ability to decrease micro leakage. Micro leakage refers to the small gaps that can occur between the restoration and the tooth, leading to potential decay. Acid etching increases the surface area and creates a rough texture, which enhances the adhesion of the bonding agent to the tooth, thus decreasing micro leakage.", "answer_choice": "A"}
